mobileRiskNotes: Keep the PNG textless; all mechanism language must stay in HTML.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the opening visual reduces mystery without implying hidden powers.

mobileRiskNotes: Do not add more branch definitions inside the tree.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the map prevents using AI, ML, generative AI, and LLM interchangeably.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep loss explanation in callout, not as a paragraph inside the board.
notesForHumanTesters: Watch whether learners understand loss as a training signal.

mobileRiskNotes: Five is the maximum; do not add side notes inside the loop.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid implying source documents are individually retrieved from weights.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the visual suggests scale without memorization.

mobileRiskNotes: Validation markers must remain readable at 320px.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep current-run details in callouts rather than adding more labels.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether durable/current-run boundary is clear after five seconds.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid morality imagery that makes the model seem conscious.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the visual feels like behavior shaping rather than moral awakening.

mobileRiskNotes: Do not add more state names inside the diagram.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether fixed versus temporary is visible.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep panel padding compact and tokens wrapped.

mobileRiskNotes: Long token rows must wrap without horizontal overflow.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether floor plus punctuation is understandable.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep numeric labels large enough.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether ID versus meaning is clear.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid making the table look like searchable source memory.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether durable table versus temporary vector is clear.

mobileRiskNotes: Distributed side may feel abstract; tester feedback should guide simplification.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the distributed side helps or just looks decorative.

mobileRiskNotes: Axis labels must stay outside crowded cells.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the batch sheet clarifies or distracts.

mobileRiskNotes: Arc endpoints must remain visually attached to chips.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether not-awareness is clear without clutter.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid adding feature names to the bars.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether attention versus MLP is separable.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep block text short; no layer-by-layer story.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether it feels mechanical rather than human-thought-like.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep embedding and hidden-state labels visibly distinct.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether hidden state sounds temporary, not memory.

mobileRiskNotes: Do not add decimals too small to read.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether raw-score versus probability is clear.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep percentages readable and avoid truth-meter styling.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether learners see that softmax does not choose.

mobileRiskNotes: Selected token must not collide with bars.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether likely-not-true lands.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep loop arrow obvious without crossing text.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether append-repeat is clear.

mobileRiskNotes: Cards must remain readable without many words.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether context versus memory is repaired.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep retriever and model visually separate.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether RAG is not training.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid too many source-card labels.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether grounding is helpful but not a truth guarantee.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep bridge metaphor from looking like a guarantee.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether hallucination differs from lying.

mobileRiskNotes: Watch whether learners distinguish evaluation from the four visual modes using callouts.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether learners can say which modes change weights.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid image-like details that distract from mechanism contrast.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether diffusion and autoregression feel distinct.

mobileRiskNotes: Video and code are explained in callouts rather than added as extra labels.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether it teaches multimodal without implying human senses.

mobileRiskNotes: Do not put the convergence caption inside the diagram.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether convergence, not spark, is obvious.

mobileRiskNotes: Later atmospheric image could improve warmth, but current coded flow is readable.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether it raises provenance without feeling accusatory or decorative.

notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether costs feel countable without fearmongering.

mobileRiskNotes: Avoid listing too many risks inside the visual.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether risk feels practical rather than spooky.

mobileRiskNotes: Later atmospheric image could add warmth, but current coded visual is readable.
notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether benefits feel bounded, not hype.

notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether human responsibility is visually central.

mobileRiskNotes: Keep card labels short and avoid overpacking the tray.
